question: A company tracks copies of a board game sold each year. Based on the chart, which of the following statements is best supported by the data shown?

options.A: It is not possible to determine the full-year trend for the most recent year from this chart alone.
options.B: The most recent year clearly has the lowest total copies sold of the entire period.
options.C: The chart proves that copies sold will continue to decline in future years.
options.D: The most recent year shows a sharp drop in sales compared with previous years.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 5 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (493 copies) directly to full-year values like 1,140 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
